front: Poem: 'Two vast and trunkless legs of stone / Stand in the desert'
Poem: 'The hand that mock'd' and 'the heart that fed' — sculptor's understanding
Poem: speaker urges wind to 'Drive my dead thoughts over the universe' / 'quicken a new birth'
back: Ode to the West Wind (Percy Bysshe Shelley)

front: Poem: wind compared to 'the bright hair uplifted from the head / Of some fierce Maenad'
back: Ode to the West Wind (Percy Bysshe Shelley)

front: Poem: speaker once 'tameless, and swift, and proud,' now weighed down by 'a heavy weight of hours'
back: Ode to the West Wind (Percy Bysshe Shelley)

front: Poem: dead man's soul 'like a star, / beacons from the abode where the Eternal are'
Poem: 'Go thou to Rome' passage — 'Life, like a dome of many-coloured glass / Stains the white radiance of Eternity'
Poem: mentions Chatterton who 'rose pale, his solemn agony... not yet faded from him'
Poem: said to predict the poet's own death by shipwreck — 'My spirit's bark is driven, / Far from the shore'
Poem: Ginsberg recalls reading 'last triumphant stanzas' in 'Kaddish'
Poem: Kerouac quoted the opening lament
Poem: skylark called a 'Poet hidden / In the light of thought'
Poem: 'profuse strains of unpremeditated art'
Poem: 'Teach me half the gladness / That thy brain must know'
Poem: bird 'lovest, but ne'er knew love's sad satiety'
Poem: Noel Coward titled Blithe Spirit from this poem's opening 'Hail to thee, blithe Spirit!'
Poem: figure on white horse 'splashed with blood' declares 'I AM GOD, AND KING, AND LAW!'
back: The Masque of Anarchy (Percy Bysshe Shelley)

front: Poem: 'maniac maid' named Hope who 'looked more like Despair'; her father 'weak and gray'
back: The Masque of Anarchy (Percy Bysshe Shelley)

front: Poem: ends 'Ye are many — they are few'
back: The Masque of Anarchy (Percy Bysshe Shelley)

front: Poem: 'Rise like lions'

front: Poem: 'shake your chains to earth, like dew'
back: The Masque of Anarchy (Percy Bysshe Shelley)

front: Play: fourth act features masque for protagonist's wedding to Asia
Play: Demogorgon declares 'this is alone Life, Joy, Empire and Victory'
Play: preface describes protagonist as 'exempt from the taints of ambition,' unlike Satan in Paradise Lost
Author: essay argues 'poets are the unacknowledged legislators of the world'
Author: written in response to Peacock's 'Four Ages of Poetry'
Poem: 'The everlasting universe of things / Flows through the mind'
Poem: 'Thou hast a voice, great Mountain'
Poem: written in Vale of Chamouni
Poem: addresses 'the awful shadow of some unseen Power'
back: Hymn to Intellectual Beauty (Percy Bysshe Shelley)

front: Poem: speaker recalls 'while yet a boy I sought for ghosts'
back: Hymn to Intellectual Beauty (Percy Bysshe Shelley)

front: Play: Beatrice hires Olimpio and Marzio to kill tyrannical father Count Francesco; walks to execution at end

front: Author: modeled as Scythrop Glowry in Peacock's Nightmare Abbey
Author: 'Mutability' — 'Naught may endure but Mutability'
Author: humans compared to 'clouds that veil the midnight moon'
Author: 'Music, When Soft Voices Die' — 'Love itself shall slumber on'; dead violets and roses
Author: Queen Mab — 'philosophical poem' dedicated to Harriet Westbrook
Author: 'How wonderful is Death / Death and his brother Sleep!'
Author: 'Alastor' — man rejects 'Arab maiden' for imagined 'veiled maid'
Author: 'Art and eloquence... are frail and vain'
Poem: written as part of competition with friend Horace Smith
Poem: Adrian Veidt (Watchmen) named after this poem
Author: published The Necessity of Atheism with Thomas Jefferson Hogg — expelled from Oxford
